tluaNumberKeyIndexDisjoint.tlua(22,13): error TLUA7053: Element implicitly has an 'any' type because expression of type '0' can't be used to index type 'StrOnly'.
  Property '0' does not exist on type 'StrOnly'.


==== tluaNumberKeyIndexDisjoint.tlua (1 errors) ====
    // Number and string index signatures are fully independent and may coexist
    // with no "number index must be assignable to string index" constraint.
    interface Both {
      [k: number]: string;
      [k: string]: number;
    }
    declare b: Both;
    local n: string = b[1];    // number index → string
    local s: number = b["x"];  // string index → number
    
    // A number index signature serves number-key access.
    interface NumOnly {
      [k: number]: boolean;
    }
    local nb: boolean = (({} as NumOnly))[0];
    
    // A string-only index does NOT serve number-key access (disjoint).
    interface StrOnly {
      [k: string]: boolean;
    }
    declare so: StrOnly;
    local bad = so[0]; // error: number key not covered by string index
                ~~~~~
!!! error TLUA7053: Element implicitly has an 'any' type because expression of type '0' can't be used to index type 'StrOnly'.
!!! error TLUA7053:   Property '0' does not exist on type 'StrOnly'.
    local okStr: boolean = so["x"];
    
    // Value-type mismatches between the two signatures are independent, not
    // forced to agree (TS requires the number value assignable to the string
    // value; tlua does not).
    interface Mixed {
      [k: number]: string;
      [k: string]: boolean;
    }
    declare m: Mixed;
    local mn: string = m[0];
    local ms: boolean = m["x"];
    
    local es = {n, s, nb, bad, okStr, mn, ms};
